title: Type Operators, Mapped Types, Conditional Types, and Templates · Spiral Review
summary: Derive form, event, and patch contracts from one domain model without duplicating names or accepting impossible states. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a derived contract and event-type library after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use type-context typeof to derive from runtime declarations without confusing it with runtime typeof.
Map over property keys with modifiers, key remapping, and value transforms while preserving intended optionality.
Use distributive and non-distributive conditional types with explicit true and false branch meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Typeof Type Context · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — Derive public patch, readonly view, and event-name types from a service model with id, status, and retryCount. A newly added region field must flow through the intended transform without exposing internal secret fields. Competency probe: Use type-context typeof to derive from runtime declarations without confusing it with runtime typeof.
